name: Trustworthy prediction
capability: Foundation MLIPs hit DFT-equivalent accuracy at 100x speed; the bottleneck moves from 'can we simulate?' to 'can we trust it?'
lupine_role: Lupine ships the audit substrate (atlas-distill engine + IMMI 2026 paper on prediction-error geometry) — the methodology that names where simulations fail and how to compress that failure into closed-loop self-correction.
reference_programs: MACE, NequIP, MatterSim, ORB, OMat24, eqV2, SevenNet; OpenKIM benchmarks; Materials Project; CHIPS Act materials qual
notes: WE ARE HERE. The eight foundation MLIP families released 2022-2025 represent the first generation of broadly-applicable atomistic ML. They saturate average-case benchmarks but fail in structured ways on production trajectories — exactly the regime our methodology characterizes.

name: Generative matter
capability: Foundation models that propose novel matter configurations from desired properties; active-learning loops close the prediction-fitting gap
lupine_role: The audit layer becomes existential. Generative models hallucinate; physical reality doesn't. Lupine's UQ + causal-error detection is what separates a generative proposal from a manufacturable molecule.
reference_programs: DeepMind GNoME (Nov 2023, 381K stable structures); Microsoft MatterSim; Meta OMat24; FAIR-Chem; Mat3ra; XtalPi
notes: Generative models for matter are roughly where AlphaFold-1 was in 2018: clearly working, not yet trustworthy enough to deploy. The audit substrate is what makes step 3 possible.

name: Closed-loop autonomous synthesis
capability: Self-driving laboratories that propose, synthesize, and characterize novel matter at the molecule and crystal scales; cycle time drops from years to days
lupine_role: Lupine becomes the verification layer between generative proposal and experimental synthesis — every synthesis is a labeled training example for the next generation of foundation models.
reference_programs: NSF Materials Innovation Platforms (MIPs); DARPA SURGE/PRIME; ARPA-E CATALCHEM-E; MGI 2031 mandate; Toyota TRI; A-Lab Berkeley
notes: Self-driving labs are the active frontier of materials research. The Materials Genome Initiative's 20-year mandate (2011-2031) targets this transition. Lupine's open-core methodology becomes the shared verification protocol.

name: Programmable matter
capability: Distributed atomic-precision manufacturing for narrow material classes (drugs polymers alloys semiconductors food substrates); the Replicator hardware exists for designed matter
lupine_role: The audit substrate is now infrastructure — like TCP/IP for matter. Lupine's open-core position means the methodology compounds across every closed-loop design system on the planet.
reference_programs: Drexler atomic-precision manufacturing roadmap; DARPA Atoms-to-Product (legacy); national lab additive manufacturing programs
notes: Programmable matter for narrow material classes is plausible on a 20-30 year horizon. General-purpose atomic-precision manufacturing (Drexler-class) is more speculative but no longer outside serious research conversation.
